mission.title: Install and Configure Metallb on Kubernetes
mission.description: MetalLB is a network load-balancer implementation for Kubernetes using standard routing protocols, ideal for bare metal clusters. Installing MetalLB allows you to expose services externally with load balancing capabilities.

steps.title: Add the MetalLB Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official MetalLB Helm repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add metallb https://metallb.github.io/metallb
```

steps.title: Update Helm repositories
steps.description:
Ensure you have the latest charts by updating your Helm repositories:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install MetalLB using Helm
steps.description:
Install MetalLB in the `metallb-system` namespace with the following command:
```bash
helm install metallb metallb/metallb --version 0.15.3 --namespace metallb-system --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the MetalLB pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metallb-system
```

steps.title: Configure MetalLB for Layer 2 mode
steps.description:
Create an IPAddressPool and L2Advertisement to configure MetalLB. Replace `192.168.1.240-192.168.1.250` with your desired IP range:
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: IPAddressPool
metadata:
  name: default-pool
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  addresses:
  - 192.168.1.240-192.168.1.250
---
apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: L2Advertisement
metadata:
  name: default-l2
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  ipAddressPools:
  - default-pool
EOF
```

uninstall.title: Remove the MetalLB Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall MetalLB with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall metallb --namespace metallb-system
```

uninstall.title: Delete the MetalLB namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the `metallb-system` namespace to clean up any remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace metallb-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
uninstall.description:
Remove the MetalLB CRDs:
```bash
kubectl delete crd bgppeers.metallb.io ipaddresspools.metallb.io
```

upgrade.title: Backup current configuration
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current MetalLB configuration:
```bash
kubectl get ipaddresspool,l2advertisement -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest charts by updating your Helm repositories:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade MetalLB
upgrade.description:
Upgrade MetalLB to the latest version with the following command:
```bash
helm upgrade metallb metallb/metallb --version 0.15.3 --namespace metallb-system
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the MetalLB pods are running successfully after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metallb-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see MetalLB pods in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n metallb-system
```
Common issues may include misconfiguration in the ConfigMap or network issues.

troubleshooting.title: MetalLB not assigning IPs
troubleshooting.description:
If MetalLB is not assigning IPs, ensure that the IPAddressPool is correctly configured and the IP range is valid and not overlapping with existing services:
```bash
kubectl get ipaddresspool -n metallb-system -o yaml
kubectl get l2advertisement -n metallb-system -o yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If services are not reachable, check the service type and ensure it is set to `LoadBalancer`:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n <your-namespace>
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of MetalLB will have all pods running in the `metallb-system` namespace and the ability to expose services with load balancing. You can verify the configuration by checking the assigned IPs and service accessibility.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: IPAddressPool
metadata:
  name: default-pool
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  addresses:
  - 192.168.1.240-192.168.1.250
---
apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: L2Advertisement
metadata:
  name: default-l2
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  ipAddressPools:
  - default-pool
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed.
